pattern: Walk the string in pairs, shifting each letter at an even index forward by the digit that follows it to rebuild the result.
hints: Even positions already hold the final letters; only the odd positions need to be transformed.
Each digit means: take the previous letter and advance it that many places in the alphabet.
Iterate over odd indices, compute chr(ord(s[i-1]) + int(s[i])), and join the original letters with the shifted ones.

edge_inputs.description: single letter with no digit to process
shift of zero keeps the letter unchanged
consecutive even shifts produce a run
shift uses the immediately preceding letter, not a running base
largest shift that stays within the alphabet
letter at the very end with no trailing digit
different letters with the same digit shift independently

stress.description: random even-letter odd-digit string with shifts kept within the alphabet
stress.note: even indices are letters up to 'u', odd are small digits so shifts never exceed 'z'
